Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile fetch, parse, normalize, atomic commit, validators, checkpoints, and post-commit effects; untrusted XML and feed content must not cross admission or rendering boundaries; then change one RSS, Atom, namespace, date, identity, content, or encoding case and reconcile provenance. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
